skill.name: manage-linkedin-network
skill.description: Turn your LinkedIn network into a searchable, annotated CRM — find who you know at any company, prep for meetings, and never lose context on a connection again. Powered by Rarefriend. Use when the user wants to: search LinkedIn connections, find who they know at a company, add notes after LinkedIn conversations or networking events, tag connections, track warm intros, recall context before reaching out, prepare for a meeting with a connection, who should I reach out to, or set up LinkedIn sync. Triggers on: LinkedIn, connections, my network, LinkedIn contacts, who do I know at, networking, professional network, warm intro, notes about someone, tag contacts, relationship context, find contact, network management, LinkedIn sync, intro, reach out, who to contact.
skill.summary: Transform your LinkedIn network into a searchable CRM with notes, tags, and relationship context.
skill.author: Whitefield-Labs

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate LinkedIn CRM integration using the Rarefriend MCP service. All static findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The 'external_commands' findings refer to markdown code blocks showing standard MCP setup commands (npx, claude mcp add), not actual code execution. The 'network' findings are hardcoded URLs to rarefriend.com and GitHub, which are legitimate service endpoints. The 'filesystem' findings reference standard MCP config file paths (~/.cursor/mcp.json, claude_desktop_config.json). The 'weak cryptographic' findings are triggered by the phrase 'Client Secret' in OAuth credential documentation, not actual cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: OAuth credentials in documentation
low_findings.description: Setup documentation includes instructions for configuring OAuth client ID and client secret as environment variables. While this is standard practice for MCP integrations, users should ensure they use their own credentials and never commit them to version control.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard OAuth setup pattern with placeholder values - not a security issue but a minor consideration for credential handling.

content.user_title: Turn Your LinkedIn Network Into a Searchable CRM
content.value_statement: Stop losing context on professional connections. This skill transforms your LinkedIn network into a searchable CRM with notes, tags, and relationship history — find who you know at any company in seconds and always remember how you met.
actual_capabilities: Search LinkedIn connections by name, company, role, or tag using natural language queries
Retrieve full contact profiles with company, role, location, and email information
Log meeting notes, conversation context, and follow-ups for each connection
Tag connections with custom labels like 'investor', 'founder', 'warm-intro' or 'active-deal'
Bulk tag multiple contacts matching a search query in a single workflow
Check LinkedIn sync status and guide users through initial setup or troubleshooting
limitations: Requires a Rarefriend account and MCP integration to function
LinkedIn sync requires installing the Rarefriend Chrome extension separately
Cannot directly send messages or connection requests on LinkedIn
Contacts created before MCP connection are not automatically migrated

use_cases.title: Meeting preparation
use_cases.description: Quickly find who you know at a company and review past conversation notes before any call or meeting.
use_cases.target_user: Founders and sales professionals preparing for prospect meetings

use_cases.title: Network organization
use_cases.description: Tag and categorize hundreds of LinkedIn connections to track investors, mentors, and potential collaborators.
use_cases.target_user: Startup founders managing a growing professional network

use_cases.title: Context recall
use_cases.description: Search across all notes and contacts to find when you last spoke with someone and what was discussed.
use_cases.target_user: Consultants, recruiters, and business developers tracking many relationships

prompt_templates.title: Find connections at a company
prompt_templates.prompt: Who do I know at Stripe?
prompt_templates.scenario: Quickly identify all connections at a target company before outreach

prompt_templates.title: Search by name
prompt_templates.prompt: Find Alice Zhang in my network
prompt_templates.scenario: Locate a specific person and view their full profile and notes

prompt_templates.title: Filter by tag
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all investors in my network
prompt_templates.scenario: Find all contacts matching a relationship or status tag

prompt_templates.title: Meeting prep workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm meeting Marcus Johnson next week. Find him in my network and show me our last conversation notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete meeting preparation including context recall and follow-up tracking

output_examples.input: Who do I know at Stripe?
output_examples.output:
Found 3 connections at Stripe:

1. Alice Wang — Senior PM at Stripe (LinkedIn extension)
   Last contact: Discussed API rate limits in March 2025

2. John Smith — Engineering Lead at Stripe (LinkedIn CSV import)
   Tags: vc-adjacent, warm-intro

3. Sarah Chen — Recruiter at Stripe (LinkedIn extension)
   No notes yet — consider adding context after your next interaction

output_examples.input: Find all VCs in my network
output_examples.output:
Found 8 contacts tagged 'vc' or 'investor':

1. Mike Johnson — Partner at Sequoia (LinkedIn)
   Pinned note: 'Met at All-In Summit 2024, interested in fintech deals'

2. Lisa Park — Principal at Andreessen Horowitz (LinkedIn)
   Tags: vc, crypto-focused

3. David Kim — Solo capitalist (LinkedIn)
   Last contact: Intro'd to Sarah via Mike, 2 weeks ago

output_examples.input: I'm meeting Marcus Johnson next week. Prep me for the meeting.
output_examples.output:
Meeting prep for Marcus Johnson:

**Current role:** VP Engineering at Vercel (updated 2 weeks ago)

**Relationship history:**
- Met at React Summit 2024
- Intro'd to your co-founder via mutual connection
- Last call: discussed edge functions, 3 weeks ago

**Pinned notes:**
- 'Interested in potential partnership on developer tooling'
- 'Prefers async communication, responds well to technical details'

**Suggested follow-ups:**
- Send the architecture doc you mentioned in the last call
- Confirm the intro to your DevRel lead

best_practices: Always check setup status first with the 'check setup' command to ensure MCP and LinkedIn sync are properly configured
Use source attribution to clarify whether contacts came from the Chrome extension sync or manual CSV import
Pin critical relationship context like warm intros or active deals so they appear first in your workflow
Tag contacts immediately after adding notes to build a searchable relationship graph over time
anti_patterns: Do not skip the sync status check before searching — assuming contacts are missing when sync is still running leads to confusion
Do not use generic tags like 'important' — use specific relationship or status tags that you will remember months later
Do not search without specifying source when looking for LinkedIn-specific contacts — mixing sources can return irrelevant results from email or calendar contacts

faq.question: Do I need a Rarefriend account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill requires a Rarefriend account and the Rarefriend MCP server to be configured. Sign up at rarefriend.com and follow the setup guide to get your API credentials.

faq.question: How do I sync my LinkedIn connections?
faq.answer: LinkedIn sync works through the Rarefriend Chrome extension. Install the extension from the Chrome Web Store, sign in to LinkedIn, then click 'Sync' in your Rarefriend settings. Large networks may take a few minutes to sync.

faq.question: Can I import contacts from a LinkedIn CSV export?
faq.answer: Yes. If you do not want to use the Chrome extension, you can export your LinkedIn connections as a CSV file and import them. The setup documentation includes a full guide for this process.

faq.question: Is my LinkedIn data secure?
faq.answer: Rarefriend reads your LinkedIn data locally through the Chrome extension and stores it in your Rarefriend account. The skill uses OAuth credentials that you control, and data is only sent between your MCP client and the Rarefriend service.

faq.question: Can I send messages or connection requests through this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill is for searching, organizing, and recalling context about your existing network. It does not send messages or make connection requests on LinkedIn.

faq.question: What AI tools does this skill work with?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Claude Desktop, and other Agent Skills-compatible clients that support MCP (Model Context Protocol).
